summary: Tracking issue to enable nullable reference types in SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor — already completed in PR #2505 (SkiaSharp 3.0 modernization) which added <Nullable>enable</Nullable> and restructured the project with full nullable annotations.

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: Nullable was enabled in the 3.0 modernization. The Blazor project was entirely restructured and nullable was enabled from the start of the new project structure.

fixStatus.reason: The SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor.csproj has <Nullable>enable</Nullable> since commit bc5020b9e (2023-07-30, PR #2505). All source files use nullable annotations (e.g., byte[]?, SKSurface?, GRContext?, Action<>? parameters).

analysis.summary: This is a maintainer-filed tracking issue for enabling nullable reference types in the Blazor views project. The work was completed as part of the SkiaSharp 3.0 modernization (PR #2505, merged 2023-07-30). The csproj has <Nullable>enable</Nullable> and all source files use proper nullable annotations throughout.
analysis.rationale: The issue is classified as enhancement (matching existing labels) since it tracks a code quality improvement. The area is SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor since that's the specific project. Fix status is confirmed by examining the csproj and source files — nullable is fully enabled with proper annotations on all nullable fields and parameters.

keySignals.interpretation: Filed by maintainer as part of a batch of nullable tracking issues (#2055-#2075) covering all SkiaSharp projects.

keySignals.text: Modernize the repository and prepare SkiaSharp 3.0 (#2505)
keySignals.interpretation: The 3.0 modernization PR restructured the Blazor project entirely, enabling nullable from the start.

codeInvestigation.finding: <Nullable>enable</Nullable> is present in the PropertyGroup, confirming nullable reference types are enabled project-wide.

Fields use nullable annotations: 'byte[]? pixels', 'Action<SKPaintSurfaceEventArgs>? OnPaintSurface', 'IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object>? AdditionalAttributes'. The null-forgiving operator (null!) is used for late-initialized DI/lifecycle fields.
GL-specific fields use nullable annotations: 'GRContext? context', 'GRGlInterface? glInterface', 'GRBackendRenderTarget? renderTarget', 'SKSurface? surface', 'SKCanvas? canvas'.
Internal files also use nullable annotations: 'JSObject? module'.

actionability.reason: Nullable reference types are fully enabled in SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor since the 3.0 modernization (PR #2505). The csproj has <Nullable>enable</Nullable> and all source files use proper nullable annotations.

actions.description: Update labels to reflect completed enhancement
Note that nullable was enabled in the 3.0 modernization
actions.comment:
Nullable reference types were enabled for SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor as part of the 3.0 modernization in PR #2505 (commit bc5020b9). The project has `<Nullable>enable</Nullable>` in its csproj and all source files use proper nullable annotations.

Closing as completed.

actions.description: Close as completed — nullable is fully enabled
